glossary.value: Goals refer to the total number of occasions a team or player has successfully placed the ball into the opponent’s net. This metric is used to determine the match outcome.

A player who, regardless of intention, last touches the ball prior to a goal being scored by a team-mate. An assist will be awarded for an intended distribution action regardless of an opposition touch, as long as the intended distribution action arives at the intended destination.
Attempts at goal represent the total number of shots (both on target and off target) taken by a team or player, including those that are blocked by defenders or saved by the goalkeeper.
Attempts at Goal on Target are a subset of total attempts that end within the goal frame, including shots that result in goals or require a save from the goalkeeper to prevent a goal.
The rate at which a player's total  attempts at goal result in goals.
A statistical model estimating the likelihood of a given attempt at goal resulting in a goal, based on factors like attempt location, type, assist type, and defensive pressure.
A measure of finishing efficiency, calculated as Goals divided by Expected Goals (XG), indicating how well a team converts chances relative to the quality of those chances.
Goals Conceded indicate the total number of goals a team has allowed their opponent to score.
Corners are a type of set play awarded to an attacking team when the ball fully crosses the goal line, outside the goalposts, after being last touched by a defending player.

Possession control shows the percentage of time each team is in possession of the ball, as well as a third state showing when the ball is in contest. The ball is in contest when neither team has it under control.
Passes refer to the deliberate transfer of the ball from one player to another on the same team.
Pass Completion Rate is the percentage of successfully completed passes out of the total attempted passes, indicating the accuracy of a player or team's passing.
Crosses are specific types of distributions delivered from wide areas of the pitch targetting the opponent’s penalty area, typically intended to create scoring opportunities.
Crossing Accuracy is the percentage of successfully completed crosses (those reaching the intended teammate) out of the total attempted crosses.
The number of attempted distributions or actions that aim to penetrate the opposition’s defensive line, typically intending to disrupt their team shape or create goal-scoring chances.
The completion rate of attempted linebreaks against the defensive line.
The number of successful switches of play that reach the intended teammate on the opposite side of the field. The ball must bypass two vertical pitch channels.
The percentage of successful switches of play.
Situations where a player or team forces the opponent to lose possession of the ball due to events such as pressure, tackles, or interceptions.
The duration of time it takes for a team to regain possession after losing the ball, indicating pressing efficiency.
Own Goals occur when a player inadvertently directs the ball into their own team's net, resulting in a goal being awarded to the opposing team.
The total number of pressing actions applied by a player or team in an attempt to challenge for the ball ball carrier and disrupt the opponents possession.
Defensive pressures that occur directly against the opponent in possession of the ball, aiming to provoke errors or turnovers by physically challenging for the ball.
The number of fouls a player or team commits against the opposition.
The number of fouls a player or team wins against the opposition.
Official cautions given by the referee for misconduct, recorded and tracked for disciplinary purposes.
Direct dismissals given for serious offences, resulting in a player being sent off and banned from further participation in the match.
Dismissals resulting from the accumulation of two yellow cards in the same match.
Instances where a player is penalised for being in an offside position when involved in active play at the moment the ball is played to them.
Matches in which a team does not concede any goals, typically used to evaluate defensive and goalkeeper performance.
Goals Conceded indicate the total number of goals a team has allowed their opponent to score.

The total number of successful goalkeeping actions that prevent a goal from being scored, including any attempt at goal stopped or deflected by the goalkeeper using any part of the body within the rules.

glossary.identifier: Goalkeeper Actions Inside the Penalty Area
glossary.value: All defensive interventions made by the goalkeeper within their own penalty area, including saves, punches, and claims.

glossary.identifier: Goalkeeper Actions Outside the Penalty Area
glossary.value: All defensive interventions made by the goalkeeper outside their penalty area, often involving tackles or clearances.
When a player has actively signalled, changed body shape or made a clear movement to receive the ball from a teammate.
When a player has actively signalled, changed body shape or made a clear movement to receive the ball in behind the oppositions defensive line.
When a player has actively signalled, changed body shape or made a clear movement to receive the ball in between the oppositions team shape or units.
When a player has actively signalled, changed body shape or made a clear movement to receive the ball in front of the oppositions team shape.
When a player has actively signalled, changed body shape or made a clear movement to receive the ball inside the opposition team shape.
When a player has actively signalled, changed body shape or made a clear movement to receive the ball outside the opposition team shape.
Successful receptions of the ball beyond the oppositions last defensive line, indicating threat creation.

glossary.identifier: Receptions Between Midfield And Defensive Line
glossary.value: Receptions of the ball in the space between the oppositions  midfield and defensive line, facilitating vertical progression of ther ball towards the goal.
Instances where a player receives the ball while being actively pressed or challenged by an opponent.
The average running speed of a player throughout the match, typically measured in km/h.
High-intensity runs made by a player that exceed a defined speed threshold, used to measure physical exertion.
Explosive high-speed movements, generally measured above a set sprint threshold.
The highest velocity reached by a player during the match. For a Player or Team collectively.
The cumulative distance a player covers during a match, recorded in meters or kilometers, across all speeds and intensities. For a Player or Team collectively.
